name: AFT Survival Regression
description: AFT Survival Regression is a parametric survival analysis model used to estimate time-to-event outcomes when data may be censored.
output: It generates the AFTSurvivalRegressionModel and passes it to the next Predict and ModelSave Nodes. The input DataFrame is also passed along to the next nodes.

fields.name: featuresCol
fields.title: Features Column
fields.description: Vector column containing input features used to estimate survival time.

fields.name: labelCol
fields.title: Label Column
fields.description: Target column representing the observed survival time.

fields.name: splitRatio
fields.title: Split Ratio
fields.description: Controls how the dataset is divided between training and validation.

fields.name: censorCol
fields.title: Censor Column
fields.description: Indicates whether the event occurred. A value of 1.0 represents an observed event, while 0.0 represents censored data.

fields.name: fitIntercept
fields.title: Fit Intercept
fields.description: Determines whether an intercept term is included in the survival regression model.

fields.name: maxIter
fields.title: Maximum Iterations
fields.description: Maximum number of optimization iterations used during model training.

fields.name: tol
fields.title: Tolerance
fields.description: Convergence threshold that determines when the training process stops.

fields.name: quantileProbabilities
fields.title: QuantileProbabilities
fields.description: Comma-separated list of probabilities used to compute survival time quantiles.

fields.name: quantilesCol
fields.title: Quantiles Column
fields.description: Column that stores estimated survival time quantiles for each observation.

fields.name: predictionCol
fields.title: Prediction Column
fields.description: Column containing predicted survival time values generated by the model.
